# Paper

I only want the H58 assemblies from this paper:
https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanmic/article/PIIS2666-5247(22)00093-3/fulltext

So I downloaded the supp tables from that paper:
* `mmc2.xlsx` - genomes sequenced for the paper
* `mmc3.xlsx` - genomes from other studies used in the paper

I then took the samples with a genotype starting with '4.3.1' (2474 from mmc2, 2416 from mmc3). Two samples didn't have run numbers and seven didn't have years, leaving me with 4881.





# Prep directories

```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper
mkdir S_enterica_Typhi_431

cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431
mkdir reads
mkdir assemblies
mkdir alignments
```

```bash
wget --retry-connrefused --waitretry=1 --read-timeout=20 --timeout=15 -t 0 -O SAMN10597673_2.fastq.gz ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/SRR832/008/SRR8322028/SRR8322028_2.fastq.gz
wget --retry-connrefused --waitretry=1 --read-timeout=20 --timeout=15 -t 0 -O SAMN10873457_1.fastq.gz ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/SRR853/005/SRR8530445/SRR8530445_1.fastq.gz
wget --retry-connrefused --waitretry=1 --read-timeout=20 --timeout=15 -t 0 -O SAMN10873457_2.fastq.gz ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/SRR853/005/SRR8530445/SRR8530445_2.fastq.gz
wget --retry-connrefused --waitretry=1 --read-timeout=20 --timeout=15 -t 0 -O SAMN10921753_1.fastq.gz ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/SRR856/007/SRR8568117/SRR8568117_1.fastq.gz
wget --retry-connrefused --waitretry=1 --read-timeout=20 --timeout=15 -t 0 -O SAMN10921753_2.fastq.gz ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/SRR856/007/SRR8568117/SRR8568117_2.fastq.gz
wget --retry-connrefused --waitretry=1 --read-timeout=20 --timeout=15 -t 0 -O SAMN10938816_1.fastq.gz ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/SRR857/006/SRR8575656/SRR8575656_1.fastq.gz
wget --retry-connrefused --waitretry=1 --read-timeout=20 --timeout=15 -t 0 -O SAMN10938816_2.fastq.gz ftp://ftp.sra.ebi.ac.uk/vol1/fastq/SRR857/006/SRR8575656/SRR8575656_2.fastq.gz
```

Three of the read sets weren't in the EBI FTP for some reason, so I had to get them from NCBI:
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads
fasterq-dump --qual-defline '+' ERR5311387
mv ERR5311387_1.fastq SAMEA6264640_1.fastq
mv ERR5311387_2.fastq SAMEA6264640_2.fastq
fasterq-dump --qual-defline '+' ERR5311402
mv ERR5311402_1.fastq SAMEA6264641_1.fastq
mv ERR5311402_2.fastq SAMEA6264641_2.fastq
fasterq-dump --qual-defline '+' ERR5311353
mv ERR5311353_1.fastq SAMEA6265033_1.fastq
mv ERR5311353_2.fastq SAMEA6265033_2.fastq
for f in *.fastq; do gzip "$f" &; done
```

And I excluded one read set due to problems: SAMEA2781410 (ERR629455) only had 36-bp unpaired reads and was labelled as _Saccharomyces_.

This left me with 4880 read sets.

Sanity check that the downloads look good (`_1.fastq.gz` and `_2.fastq.gz` files have the same number of reads):
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads
for s in $(ls *_1.fastq.gz | sed 's/_1.fastq.gz//'); do
    r1_count=$(fast_count "$s"_1.fastq.gz | cut -f2)
    r2_count=$(fast_count "$s"_2.fastq.gz | cut -f2)
    if [[ "$r1_count" == "$r2_count" ]]; then
        echo $s"\t"$r1_count"\tmatch"
    else
        echo $s"\t"$r1_count"\t"$r2_count"\tMISMATCH"
    fi
done
```




# Unicycler assemblies

```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
for r1 in /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads/*_1.fastq.gz; do
    r2=${r1/_1.fastq.gz/_2.fastq.gz}
    s=$(echo "$r1" | grep -oP "SAM[EAN]+\d+")
    cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
    mkdir "$s"; cd "$s"
    sbatch --job-name="$s" --time=0-4:00:00 --partition=genomics --qos=genomics --ntasks=1 --mem=64000 --cpus-per-task=8 --account md52 --wrap "module load spades/3.15.3; module load blast+/2.9.0; fastp --in1 "$r1" --in2 "$r2" --out1 illumina_1.fastq.gz --out2 illumina_2.fastq.gz --unpaired1 illumina_u.fastq.gz --unpaired2 illumina_u.fastq.gz; /home/rwic0002/programs/Unicycler/unicycler-runner.py -1 illumina_1.fastq.gz -2 illumina_2.fastq.gz -s illumina_u.fastq.gz -o unicycler --threads 8; rm illumina_*.fastq.gz"
done
```

Some assemblies failed because the unpaired file was empty (which SPAdes didn't like), so I reassembled those:
```bash
for s in SAMN03465688 SAMN03465709 SAMN03465840 SAMN03466108 SAMN03466115 SAMN03466176 SAMN03466454 SAMN03466511 SAMN03466550 SAMN03468457 SAMN03469878 SAMN03469895 SAMN03473946 SAMN03473949 SAMN03473978 SAMN03474073 SAMN03475595 SAMN03475662 SAMN03475770 SAMN03475959 SAMN03476114 SAMN03476119 SAMN03476172 SAMN03476207 SAMN03476350 SAMN03476761 SAMN03477271 SAMN03477483 SAMN03477558 SAMN03477593 SAMN03477605 SAMN03477628 SAMN03477728 SAMN03477828 SAMN03477831 SAMN03478039 SAMN03478112 SAMN03478285 SAMN03478371 SAMN03478529 SAMN03479146 SAMN03479957 SAMN03479983 SAMN03480112 SAMN03480243 SAMN04362935 SAMN04363012 SAMN04363111 SAMN04363116 SAMN04363218 SAMN04363326 SAMN04363361 SAMN04363435 SAMN04363587 SAMN04363649 SAMN04363872 SAMN04363880 SAMN04363987 SAMN04364057 SAMN04364181; do
    cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
    rm -r "$s"
    mkdir "$s"; cd "$s"
    r1=/projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads/"$s"_1.fastq.gz
    r2=/projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads/"$s"_2.fastq.gz
    sbatch --job-name="$s" --time=0-4:00:00 --partition=genomics --qos=genomics --ntasks=1 --mem=64000 --cpus-per-task=8 --account md52 --wrap "module load spades/3.15.3; module load blast+/2.9.0; fastp --in1 "$r1" --in2 "$r2" --out1 illumina_1.fastq.gz --out2 illumina_2.fastq.gz --unpaired1 illumina_u.fastq.gz --unpaired2 illumina_u.fastq.gz; /home/rwic0002/programs/Unicycler/unicycler-runner.py -1 illumina_1.fastq.gz -2 illumina_2.fastq.gz -o unicycler --threads 8; rm illumina_*.fastq.gz"
done
```




# Read and assembly stats

Total read bases:
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads
for r1 in *_1.fastq.gz; do
    r2=${r1/_1.fastq.gz/_2.fastq.gz}
    s=$(echo "$r1" | grep -oP "SAM[EAN]+\d+")
    r1bases=$(fast_count "$r1" | cut -f3)
    r2bases=$(fast_count "$r2" | cut -f3)
    totalbases=$(( $r1bases + $r2bases ))
    echo $s"\t"$totalbases
done
```

fastp filtering:
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
for s in SAM*; do
    r1_bases_before=$(cat "$s"/slurm-*.out | grep -A2 "Read1 before filtering:" | grep "total bases:" | grep -oP "\d+")
    r2_bases_before=$(cat "$s"/slurm-*.out | grep -A2 "Read2 before filtering:" | grep "total bases:" | grep -oP "\d+")
    r1_bases_after=$(cat "$s"/slurm-*.out | grep -A2 "Read1 after filtering:" | grep "total bases:" | grep -oP "\d+")
    r2_bases_after=$(cat "$s"/slurm-*.out | grep -A2 "Read2 after filtering:" | grep "total bases:" | grep -oP "\d+")
    printf $s"\t"
    echo $r1_bases_before"\t"$r2_bases_before"\t"$r1_bases_after"\t"$r2_bases_after | awk '{print ($3+$4)/($1+$2);}'
done
```

Assembly size (from fasta):
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
for s in SAM*; do
    size=$(fast_count "$s"/unicycler/assembly.fasta | cut -f3)
    echo $s"\t"$size
done
```

Assembly N50 (from fasta):
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
for s in SAM*; do
    n50=$(fast_count "$s"/unicycler/assembly.fasta | cut -f6)
    echo $s"\t"$n50
done
```

Assembly contigs (from fasta):
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
for s in SAM*; do
    n50=$(fast_count "$s"/unicycler/assembly.fasta | cut -f2)
    echo $s"\t"$n50
done
```

Assembly dead ends (from gfa):
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
for s in SAM*; do
    dead=$(deadends "$s"/unicycler/assembly.gfa)
    echo $s"\t"$dead
done
```




# Species check

Just to ensure that there were no mix-ups, I checked each assembly against the Kleborate species database:
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
wget https://github.com/katholt/Kleborate/raw/main/kleborate/data/species_mash_sketches.msh
for s in SAM*; do
    printf $s"\t" >> species_results.tsv
    if [ -f "$s"/unicycler/assembly.fasta ]; then
        mash dist species_mash_sketches.msh "$s"/unicycler/assembly.fasta | sort -nk3,3 | head -n1 | cut -f1,3 | perl -pe 's|/.+\t|\t|' | sed 's/_/ /g' >> species_results.tsv
    else
        printf "\n" >> species_results.tsv
    fi
done
rm species_mash_sketches.msh
```
All came back as _Salmonella enterica_, as expected.





# QC

I rejected samples where any of the following was true:
* fastp pass rate <10% (1 assembly)
* Failed to assemble (none)
* Assembly size >6 Mbp (2 assemblies)
* Assembly N50 <15 kbp (none)
* Assembly graph dead ends >100 (7 assemblies)
* Heterogeneity (bubbles) in assembly graph (2 assemblies)

```bash
mkdir /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads/qc_fail
mkdir /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies/qc_fail

for s in SAMEA7456595 SAMEA9253021 SAMN03476558 SAMN04363286 SAMN04576219 SAMN06885540 SAMN06885550 SAMN06885591 SAMN09209733 SAMN09209770 SAMEA5188339 SAMEA7883717; do
    cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads
    mv "$s"_*.fastq.gz qc_fail
    cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
    mv "$s" qc_fail
done
```
This left me with 4868 assemblies.

Going forward, I only need the assembly FASTAs, so I'll move the Unicycler files elsewhere and just copy out the final result:
```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431
mv assemblies assemblies_working_files
mkdir assemblies
cd assemblies_working_files
for s in SAM*; do
    cp "$s"/unicycler/assembly.fasta ../assemblies/"$s".fasta
done
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies
gzip *.fasta
```





# Reference genome

```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431
wget https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/genomes/all/GCF/000/195/995/GCF_000195995.1_ASM19599v1/GCF_000195995.1_ASM19599v1_genomic.fna.gz
echo ">Reference" > Reference.fasta
seqtk seq GCF_000195995.1_ASM19599v1_genomic.fna.gz | head -n2 | tail -n1 >> Reference.fasta
rm GCF_000195995.1_ASM19599v1_genomic.fna.gz
```



# Scale trees

There is a group of random 25 isolates from a tight group in the Snippy tree (i.e. without recombination filtering), so I'm going to use them to scale the tree distances for the BactDating figure.

```bash
cd /projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies

files=(SAMEA4759379 SAMEA5188632 SAMEA5596012 SAMEA5188556 SAMEA5188787 SAMEA5188770 SAMEA5188405 SAMEA5567907 SAMEA5189066 SAMEA4759421 SAMEA5188744 SAMEA4759224 SAMEA5189153 SAMEA5189047 SAMEA5189004 SAMEA5189103 SAMEA5900026 SAMEA5188658 SAMEA5899999 SAMN03478371 SAMEA2142785 SAMEA5188649 SAMEA4759220 SAMEA5189063 SAMEA5596326)
max=${#files[@]}

for ((i=1; i<=max; i++)); do
    for ((j=i+1; j<=max; j++)); do
        catpac.py --minreldepth 0.5 --maxreldepth 1.5 "$files[$i]".fasta "$files[$j]".fasta
        printf $files[$i]"\t"$files[$j]"\t" >> snp_counts
        grep -c "SNP" catpac_variants.csv >> snp_counts
        rm catpac_*
    done
done

sort -nk3,3 snp_counts | cut -f3 | awk '{a[i++]=$1;} END {print a[int(i/2)];}'  # get the median SNP count
```

The median pairwise SNP count in this group was 28.




# Final paths

* Assemblies: `/projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/assemblies/*.fasta.gz`
* Reads: `/projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/reads/*_[12].fastq.gz`
* Reference: `/projects/js66/individuals/ryan/Verticall_paper/S_enterica_Typhi_431/Reference.fasta`
